tokens: Okay; now John (Young) would like to talk to you about the fender fix.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Hey, while John's talking to me, why don't you check my biomed out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me ask you one question here on Jack's PLSS water fill.
We're showing about 3 pounds too much water in the LM system, and we're wondering if you got the Aux tanks filled up in Jack's PLSS.
Were you sure to have the Aux valve open and did you see good clear water in the sight gauge with no bubbles after the fill?
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I guess we'd have to say "yes" to those questions; but if you've got a question on it we can go through it again.
I'd rather do that than take a chance (on running out of cooling water during the EVA).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me make sure we want to do that.
While we're making sure, I checked with both homefronts.
Nassau Bay and Tucson are both in good shape.
Geno, Tracy upstaged you for about 30 minutes last night on local TV during her own interview there, and drew everybody away from watching EVA during that time.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Hey, Gordo, why do you say "Jack's PLSS"?
Did you see the water drop in the LM when we charged mine?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It was the profile of the water quantity as you were filling both PLSSs, and it was the fill during Jack's fill that looked suspicious...like just maybe 3 pounds less than there should have been (of) flow when you were filling Jack's.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Yeah, well, you know it's only (a) passive (means) to know whether or not you've got it filled.
I sure don't want to go out there and have him just have some partial water.
So let's do the conservative thing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There was some drinking water going out at that time, too, which muddles up the data a little bit, so we're not absolutely certain on that.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: We weren't drinking water while we were filling the PLSS, however.
You come up with what you think's best on that, and I'm ready to copy John.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Geno, I don't think you need to copy this.
With your four cronopaque maps, tape two maps and allow about a l-inch overlap to (create) a 15-inch by 10-1/2-inch configuration.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And then repeat with two other maps, and then tape the two maps - now four maps - tape them together, and you'll end up with a sheet that's about 15 inches by 19 inches, a sheet of cronopaque.
And then tape both sides of it - the overlapping edges - to strengthen it.
And you can further strengthen it if you tape an "X" of tape across both sides of it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And then, on the roll up, on the long axis, secure it with a strip of tape and put it in the ETB.
And, on that strip of tape you secure it with, be sure and leave a tab on the end of it so you can get it off with your gloves.
And then remove clamps from both the utility light units, and open the clamp jaws to max.
And then tighten the mounting bracket that you've got on it so (that) it will be swinging around; and stow the clamps in the ETB.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Now you've got everything you need.
And it's all put together and all ready to be fastened to the Rover.
And then when you get the ETB in the (Rover) seat, you unroll the cronopaque sheet and you locate the front edge - with the long axis fore and aft - even with the axle.
And you lay the edge of the sheet over the inboard guide rail and you clamp it.
And you lay the other edge of the sheet over the outboard guide rail and clamp it.
And, as I said, the inboard clamp must be directly over the axle to avoid interference while steering.
And tighten the clamps securely, both of them.
And then while you're driving around out there by yourself, it would be good if Jack could take a look at it and see if you're getting any unusual dynamics.
And at Station 2, you should inspect the fender for any unusual wear that might have been caused by this mass out there on the fender, of those clamps bouncing up and down.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: One thing about it, doing it in a suit, Gene, you have to push in with your leg and hold; and it's sort of a two-handed job.
And I'm not sure in one-sixth g if you can position the fender, the pseudofender, on there without Jack, say, holding on to the long end behind the Rover so that won't fall off.
It works okay in one g for one man.
But I'm not sure it's not a two-man proposition in one-sixth (g).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We'll take a look at it, babe.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and you really have to bear down to get those things (the clamps) on the dovetail (guide rail) there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I just want to make sure of the geometry now.
We want to take two of those pages and put the 10-inch sides together overlapping, right?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Then take two more and put the 10-inch sides together overlapping, right?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And then take those two pieces you've got now and put them end to end, so you've got a long fender.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, you end up with all four pieces in a big rectangle.
You've got a 15-inch by 19-inch sheet of paper.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And I copy the overlap and everything.
If you had no overlap, I guess you'd have about 16 by 20.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But you need to overlap, and taping both sides of it gives it more strength, which you need in that situation.
(Pause) You just want to make sure it's not more than an inch, or you won't have enough to cover up those dovetails.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, babe, we're going to work on it right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Jack and Gene, this is Gordo again.
On the PLSS charge, we're recommending you hook up Jack's according to the decal.
Go right ahead and...Stand by one.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Go by the decal and do the full 5-minute fill on Jack's PLSS.
It'll take that long to get the Aux tanks filled up, if it was indeed empty.
And maybe that's something you can start and (also) work on the paper (cronopaque) taping.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I'm on step 4 on the decal...(Correcting himself) Step 5 on the decal.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: John Covington advises that the sight gauge is not a certain indicator that you're filled; and so we're just going to go by time to be sure, and kind of disregard the sight gauge readings - as a positive indication anyway.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, understand that from the past, and we went exactly by time before.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll see if anybody timed you here.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: And we did not see any water flow to speak of, so it probably was full.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Yeah, the condensate indications here were that it was full.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We want to be sure to have one look at your Biomed before you get into the suit in case something's wrong with it.
And if you go right by the checklist, we'll miss that look.
So when you get to a convenient place, if you can go to Left and have us take a look at it, we'd appreciate it.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: (Pause) I called it out, (but) I didn't give it to you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You can press on with the suiting operation, there.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: (Pause) Call me the little old fender maker.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: With respect to the PLSS water fill, last thing we heard you say was "doing step 7".
We just want to verify that you did go ahead and do step 8, which is connect the waste management system to the PLSS Aux vent for 10 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Yes, that was all done, Gordy.
We just got side-tracked, and I didn't call you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack's coming up (on comm), and I'm going off the air.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Reading the Personal Radiation Dosimeter numbers slowly, one at a time) 24128.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) How come you guys are not on "Flight Director" like I am?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, how do you read the Biomed on the LMP?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think that means "loud and clear".
(Pause) And, Challenger, have you changed your
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I guess we missed that in the checklist.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's sort of towards the end of (page)
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, I guess we're not quite there yet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, how do you read me?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're still trying to acquire the High Bit Rate.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Geno, we have good data from you on the Surgeon.
(Pause) And we have High Bit Rate.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I got good data up here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, we're Go to do the battery management now, Jack.
We've got the High Bit Rate.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And a thought for the day.
We're not sure that there is going to be any need for the
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're going to take them out with us.
So just make a note that we bring them back in, would you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll make a little note again.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Battery's complete, and (I'm waiting for) your cue on (going to) the Low Bit Rate.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You can go Low Bit Rate again.
And it looked good to us, too.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: LMP is (pause) sixty-one hundred (6100 psi) on the OPS.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And about fifty-eight fifty (5850) on CDR.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And you guys got the word about which purge valves to use?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Bob, the LMP has his OPS on.
(Correcting himself) Would you believe the PLSS?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And I bet the CDR's doing his now.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: We're in the top of the right-hand column (on the EVA cue card).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, how do you read Commander on VOX?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Your PLSS PTT (Push-to-Talk) to main, right, verify?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You'll get a tone, a vent flag, a press flag, and an O2 (oxygen) flag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Slightly garbled) Press flag, tone, vent flag.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Give Houston a call, and give them your oxygen reading.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This is the LMP with ninety-three percent.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We read you loud and garbled just like last night when the
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Houston, we had a Master Alarm, ECS.
(Pause) Could be we've got a little pumping in the ECS system...in the hoses.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're seeing it as the Water Separator.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You can pull the Water Separator circuit breaker...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (To Jack) You don't have a bleed on yours.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) I think we've fixed it.
I had the hoses in my storage slot.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Houston, CDR is reading ninety...(Refining his reading) ninety-one percent.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You go B, and I'll go A.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Houston, how do you read CDR?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I read you loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: You got an O flag and a vent flag, press flag and...?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Houston, how do you read CDR?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And how do you read the LMP?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...good PLSS data for both of you.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: "On (circuit breaker panel) 16, ECS LGC (sic; means LCG) Pump (circuit breaker), Closed." Why don't you close it again.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: "Verify ECS Caution and O2 and Water Sep lights come on in about a minute." Okay.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: "Connect your OPS hose PGA blue to blue."
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: OBS (sic) going to PGA, and I'll turn around and let you...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...make sure I get that, because it's under (garbled) connector.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Did you already get...Let's see, where are we here?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Master Alarm and ECS light; Water Sep light.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It is locked, (dust) cover is on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And you are Low (flow) and you are in and you are locked.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You get to do the same.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Okay, it's in there (that is, the OPS hose is connected) and it's locked, (garbled), and the dust cover is on.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And now, we get to put your Purge (valve) in: 208.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It is, and Low, and the pin is in.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's get another zap of (drinking) water here.
(Long Pause) If I have any more water I'll float out there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Be a good place to fill with water, you'd make a nice rec(reation) site out of this valley.
You could put some cabins up on the side of the massifs.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The fishing ought to be pretty good if you stocked it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Have a Bear Island and a Family Island.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Laughs) We're going to (have to) fill up the other end though, so it doesn't drain out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Lock it and turn your Descent Water, Off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Snaps, snaps, snaps; the whole world is held together with snaps!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I've got my hand lubed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, before we turn the (PLSS) fans on and use (PLSS) battery power...Let's just look ahead; we got helmets ready to go...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, more or less a little far out, but I think...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Make sure you've got the plug out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: My end's right, now, but (garbled) even get it out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Feel a little pressure in there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And then we'll lower our protective visor and secure tool harness and self-doff straps.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You can turn your...Let me get your
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you ready (for your helmet and
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The alignment is way over here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think it's caught on the
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Get it all the way off.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Looks like it's going to be much better.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Want to make sure that thing is on!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, (do you) want me to squat down?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I tell you, I got my fingers on it all the way around.
Better put your (PLSS) fan on here pretty quick.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let me get you dressed up back here.
(Garbled) center doesn't work, you're going to keep this thing...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's your OPS; that's your inlet; that's your oxygen or your exhaust; and (that's) your purge valve.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, let me take a look at all yours.
The main thing is to get this stuff back over here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's hard to see with that visor on there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, she's in the engaged position here, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Circuit breaker; white dots plus EVA decals.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you give me a little room to turn?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Leave the (ECS LCG) pump on for a minute.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; and EVA decals, all right?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Geno, we don't see your (PLSS) fan on.
If you've got your helmet on, you ought to have your fan on.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Boy, grease and lunar dust really make a nice mobile graphite material.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm locked on the right, verified.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and I've got my cover (glove) on over here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I'm getting one of them anyway.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can get the other one for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) (Garbled) And it's on, and locked and locked.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I may be learning how on these, finally.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If I can get my black band on here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think I'm learning how, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Must be easier in one-sixth g.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Punning) Don't throw down a gauntlet, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, and did you disconnect your (water hose)...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let's turn around and let's help each other.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's still...(Kidding Gene) This 3 degrees pitch up - 5 degrees pitch up - of yours, its an awful nuisance, Cernan.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know why you don't learn how to land one of these things.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hold that for a minute...It was a pitching deck.
Locked...(although it) took a lot to "in"-ing, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) You know those chamber runs we had were probably some of the best training we ever did.
I hate to say that, because it was some work.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It was just a little sluggish.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, every time you do that, my stomach gurgles.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay...Let me turn around to stow.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You can stow that, and mine's over here.
PLSS diverter valve, Min." Want to verify that?
(Pause) And, "connect PLSS water hose, verify lock, PLSS diverter valve Min, and PLSS pump (on)." Okay?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I want to make sure this (hose) is out of the way when I come in (at the end of the EVA).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, you want my diverter Min?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Your (PLSS LCG) pump on and Press Reg's A and B, Egress.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I'm getting a little bite (in his PGA).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And mine's on (that is, his PLSS pump).
Okay, the next thing is to turn your PLSS O2 On, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: If not, I'll get it for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get it for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We going at the same time.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Do you need me to watch the panel or you got it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, no sweat I've got that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'll have to get the PLSS O2, Off (to start the integrity check).
I'll get mine; I can reach yours real easy.
I can get it at a minute.
As soon as we get up, I'll get it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I can reach it, I think, now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And we're going up to high pressure here when we start dumping the cabin.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, when you get up, you can turn yours off.
Give me a hack, and I'll check the time.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I went at 20 (seconds), you went at 30 (on the second hand of Gene's
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It looks like it's a little tighter.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That was the suit loop we were checking yesterday up in orbit, though.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, but you know we got two tenths (of a psi pressure decrease).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I'm coming down to 20 more seconds, you got 30 more seconds (to watch the suit pressure decrease).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm only about a tenth (of a psi), I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; 1 minute for me Houston; (3).85 (down) to about (3.)72.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and we'd like your Go, Robert.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Jack, (on circuit breaker panel) "16, Cabin Repress, Open; and Cabin Repress valve, Closed."
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And then stay over there (on the right side of the cabin) as far as you can, 'cause I got to get the overhead
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I'm over as far as I can get.
I can turn around and give you more room.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, turn around and you'll have to look at the Cabin (pressure gauge).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You went awful weak all of a sudden.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You got to hit your volume.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, your arm's in the way.
I can get at it now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You want to go to...Let me get that..."Open, then Auto at 3.5." Okay, go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can see it (the valve) open.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And my cuff gauge went up to 5.0.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: At 5:30, more or less (Central Standard Time in the afternoon of Tuesday 11December 1972).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt
